reason: Stage B/C PSLQ classification (n_workers=23 ProcessPoolExecutor, dps=150, 8-element trans basis, 241892 convergent tasks) ran 110+ minutes without completing pool.map(). 7 of 23 workers still active at halt time (each with ~5170s CPU = 86 min of compute per worker). Pool.map() blocks until all workers finish; no partial-result extraction is possible from the running map() call. Per spec HALT IF Total wall > 3 hr (1.5x nominal), halted at 3h13m to push partial state.

stage_bc_pslq.note: Initial 16 workers' CPU time lost when they exited cleanly; total cumulative compute likely 50-70 kCPU-s. No results were written from the pool.map() call -- it never returned.

outcome_determination.interpretation: Cannot determine A/B/C outcome from Stage A alone.

preconditions.P3_prior_harness_or_ramanujan: PASS (CLI-Synth-staged sweep_b1_5_8_9_10.py found at sessions/2026-05-08/T2B-BZERO-OFFSET-LOG-SWEEP-B5-8-9-10/, adapted from B6 dispatch)

harness_modifications_made_in_session: Added stage_a_cache.json persistence (so Stage A can be skipped on re-fire).

Replaced multiprocessing.Pool with concurrent.futures.ProcessPoolExecutor.map (Python 3.14 + Start-Process -RedirectStandardOutput triggered WinError 87 in spawn_main with mp.Pool; ProcessPoolExecutor + Start-Process WindowStyle Hidden without external redirect was stable).
Added serial-fallback path if ProcessPoolExecutor fails.
Added _redirect_to_logfile() at __main__ entry (writes stdout+stderr to run.log internally; required because Start-Process -RedirectStandardOutput broke worker spawn).

wall_budget_breakdown.first_attempt_failed: Started ~21:12, multiprocessing.Pool spawn errors with Start-Process -RedirectStandardOutput; parent died, no results.
wall_budget_breakdown.harness_repair: ~22:00-22:10 (added cache, ProcessPoolExecutor fallback, internal stdout redirection)
wall_budget_breakdown.second_attempt_started: ~22:18 launch with Start-Process WindowStyle Hidden (no external redirect)

halt_authority: Relay-prompt-spec HALT IF clause: HALT_044_WALL_BUDGET_EXCEEDED (Total wall > 3 hr / 1.5x nominal).
downstream_impact: Outcome A/B/C not determined. Synthesizer arbitration on next-step deferred. v3.1 stratification (Class A/B + Bauer orbit + b1=7 singular) NOT amended/refuted by this run -- the Outcome A 'B7_STAYS_SINGULAR_BROADER' tag, Outcome B 'CANDIDATE_4TH_LAW_2POINTS' tag, and Outcome C 'CANDIDATE_4TH_LAW_HARDEN' tag all remain untested. Re-fire 044 with restored Stage A cache (skipping ~7 min Stage A) is the obvious next step; with the harness now stable, the second attempt should hit only the PSLQ wall.
recommended_resolution: Re-fire 044 with the same harness; Stage A will load from stage_a_cache.json (saves 7 min) and Stage B/C will run from clean state.
OR: tighten Stage B/C runtime by reducing PSLQ_HMAX_TRANS from 10^9 to 10^7 (much faster on common cases, may miss some hard relations -- discuss with Synthesizer).
OR: split Stage B/C into 4 per-b1 sub-runs each capped at ~25 min wall, with intermediate checkpoint between b1 values.
